meta.architecture_style: Multi-binary Go monolith with domain-per-package organization. Seven independent binaries (server, billing-worker, balance-worker, sink-worker, notification-service, jobs, benthos-collector) share domain packages under openmeter/ assembled via Google Wire dependency injection in app/common. HTTP API is dual-versioned (v1 via api/api.gen.go + openmeter/server/router, v2/v3 via api/v3/), both generated from a TypeSpec source. Persistence is PostgreSQL through Ent ORM; analytics queries run against ClickHouse. Kafka is the event bus for ingest, cross-worker communication, and internal system events routed via Watermill.
meta.executive_summary: OpenMeter is a Go-based metering and billing platform organized as a monorepo producing seven deployable binaries that share a large set of domain packages. The domain layer (openmeter/) is strictly layered: each major domain exposes a Go interface (Service, Connector, or FeatureConnector), has a hand-written Ent/PostgreSQL adapter, and optionally an httpdriver or httphandler package that adapts the service interface to HTTP handlers using a generic httptransport.Handler[Request,Response] pattern. Application-level dependency wiring lives exclusively in app/common using Google Wire provider sets; each cmd/* binary calls a Wire-generated initializeApplication to get a fully wired app struct. The API surface is defined in TypeSpec (api/spec/) and compiled to OpenAPI YAML and then to Go server stubs via oapi-codegen; the v1 server (openmeter/server/router) and v3 server (api/v3/server) are separate packages, both generated from their respective specs. Database schema is owned by Ent (openmeter/ent/schema/) and migrations are generated by Atlas into tools/migrate/migrations/ as golang-migrate .up/.down SQL files.

components.responsibility: Main HTTP API server. Initializes the application via Wire, runs DB migration, registers namespace handlers (Ledger, Kafka Ingest), provisions the default namespace and billing profile, then mounts both the v1 router and a telemetry server and starts Kafka producers and notification event handler goroutines in a shared oklog/run group.

components.name: billing-worker
components.responsibility: Background worker that processes billing-related Kafka system events: subscription sync events, invoice creation events, and charge advancement events. Uses Watermill router with Kafka subscriber.

components.name: balance-worker
components.responsibility: Background worker that handles entitlement balance recalculation events. Subscribes to system and ingest Kafka topics via Watermill, processes batched ingest events to update metered entitlement grant burn-down, and dispatches balance threshold notifications.

components.name: sink-worker
components.responsibility: Consumes raw CloudEvents from Kafka (written by the ingest path), deduplicates them, and batch-inserts into ClickHouse. After flushing, publishes ingest notification events to Watermill so downstream workers (balance-worker) can act on new usage data.

components.name: notification-service
components.responsibility: Standalone notification dispatcher. Subscribes to system events topic via Watermill, receives entitlement balance threshold and invoice events, and delivers webhook payloads via Svix.

file_placement_rules.description: Service interfaces are declared at the package root of each domain package. The interface composes sub-interfaces (e.g., billing.Service embeds ProfileService, InvoiceService, etc.).
Adapter interfaces (data access layer) are declared at the package root alongside the service interface.
The concrete Ent-backed implementation of the domain Adapter interface lives in a sub-package named adapter/.
The concrete service struct implementing the domain Service interface lives in a service/ sub-package.

file_placement_rules.naming_pattern: httpdriver/ or httphandler/ sub-package with handler.go
file_placement_rules.description: HTTP handler interfaces and implementations live in httpdriver/ (most domains) or httphandler/ (meter, portal, subject, meterevent). These use pkg/framework/transport/httptransport.Handler for type-safe request decoding and response encoding.
v3 API endpoint handlers are organized per resource under api/v3/handlers/. Each handler package is constructed with required domain service interfaces.
Each domain area has a corresponding file in app/common/ containing Wire provider set variables (var Billing = wire.NewSet(...)) and constructor functions that instantiate adapter, then service.
Per-binary Wire set compositions live in app/common/openmeter_<binary>.go, combining the relevant domain Wire sets for that binary.
All generated files have Code generated by X, DO NOT EDIT headers. They must never be edited manually; regenerate with make generate or make gen-api.
Ent entity definitions are the source of truth for database schema. Each entity is in a separate file named after the entity.
golang-migrate format migration files generated by atlas migrate diff. Timestamps ensure ordering.
Test helper types (mock implementations, test database setup) are co-located in a testutils/ sub-package under each domain, independent from app/common.

naming_conventions.pattern: PascalCase interface named Service or <Noun>Service in domain package
naming_conventions.description: Domain service interfaces are exported as Service or <Noun>Service in the domain's root package.
Data access interfaces are named Adapter in the domain root package, composing sub-adapters (e.g., ProfileAdapter).
Older domain packages and those not yet refactored to the service/adapter pattern use Connector as the primary interface name.

naming_conventions.pattern: Handler interface in httpdriver/ or httphandler/ package
naming_conventions.description: HTTP handler packages export a Handler interface composing per-resource sub-interfaces (ProfileHandler, InvoiceHandler, etc.).
All service method inputs are structs named with <Action><Entity>Input suffix and implement models.Validator.

naming_conventions.pattern: PascalCase var named after domain in app/common
naming_conventions.description: Wire provider sets in app/common are package-level vars named after their domain.

naming_conventions.pattern: <Domain>Registry struct in app/common grouping related services
naming_conventions.description: Where multiple services in a domain need to be passed together, a registry struct is used rather than passing individual services.

naming_conventions.pattern: SystemEventsTopic, IngestEventsTopic, BalanceWorkerEventsTopic in worker Options structs
naming_conventions.description: Kafka topic references are passed as string fields in worker Options structs; topic names are configured via TopicMapping in eventbus.
All generated files use the .gen.go suffix (except wire_gen.go which follows Wire conventions).

findings.problem_statement: Dual HTTP API versioning with significant overlap. The v1 router (openmeter/server/router/) and v3 server (api/v3/server/) both serve HTTP endpoints for overlapping resources (meters, customers, subscriptions, billing, apps). The router.Config struct in v1 accepts all domain services while api/v3/server/server.go independently constructs its own handler set from the same domain services. This creates two parallel wiring paths that must be kept in sync.
findings.evidence: openmeter/server/router/router.go imports billing, customer, meter, notification, subscription and all their httpdriver packages. api/v3/server/server.go imports the same domain packages independently and creates separate handler structs (metersHandler, customersHandler, billingProfilesHandler). Both servers are mounted in cmd/server/main.go.

findings.problem_statement: app/common is a single large package with ~40 files covering all domain wiring. Adding a new domain requires modifying this shared package. The comment in openmeter_server.go ('TODO: create a separate file or package for each application instead') indicates the current approach is a known interim design.
findings.evidence: app/common contains 43 files (billing.go, customer.go, entitlement.go, subscription.go, app.go, ledger.go, llmcost.go, etc.). All binaries share the same package. The TODO comment appears in app/common/openmeter_server.go.

findings.problem_statement: feature.FeatureConnector in openmeter/productcatalog/feature has a comment '// TODO: refactor to service pattern' indicating it has not been migrated to the canonical Service/Adapter split used by newer domains.
findings.evidence: openmeter/productcatalog/feature/connector.go line 58: '// TODO: refactor to service pattern'. The interface is named FeatureConnector rather than Service and lives directly in connector.go rather than service.go.

findings.problem_statement: Credits subsystem has conditional noop wiring that must be managed across three independent layers: ledger account services/resolvers, ledger-backed customer handlers, and namespace/default-account provisioning. The AGENTS.md explicitly calls out that disabling credits requires guarding at multiple layers and that ledger account backfill must bypass the DI outputs.
findings.evidence: app/common/ledger.go wires ledger account services to noop implementations when credits.enabled=false. AGENTS.md: 'When credits.enabled is false, app/common wires ledger account services/resolvers to noop implementations. Any ledger account backfill that must write real ledger_accounts / ledger_customer_accounts rows needs to construct concrete ledger account + resolver adapters directly instead of relying on the default DI outputs.'

findings.problem_statement: Ent view entities do not appear in the generated migrate.Tables list and Atlas may report no changes for them, requiring manual SQL migrations for view DDL. This is a known limitation documented in AGENTS.md.
findings.evidence: AGENTS.md: 'schemas declared with ent.View can generate query code under openmeter/ent/db/, but they do not appear in openmeter/ent/db/migrate/schema.go or the generated migrate.Tables list. If atlas migrate --env local diff ... reports no changes for a new view, verify whether the view exists in generated migration metadata before debugging Atlas; view DDL may need an explicit SQL migration until generator support is added.'

findings.problem_statement: The subscription.SubscriptionServiceWithWorkflow struct in app/common has a comment 'TODO: break up to multiple initializers', indicating the NewSubscriptionServices constructor is doing too much in a single function and is known to need decomposition.
findings.evidence: app/common/subscription.go line 38: '// TODO: break up to multiple initializers'

patterns.name: Layered Domain Service/Adapter/Repository
patterns.when_to_use: All business-logic domains under openmeter/<domain>/. The pattern applies whenever you need to separate persistence from orchestration.
patterns.how_it_works: Each domain exposes a Go interface (e.g. billing.Service, customer.Service, notification.Service). A concrete service struct in <domain>/service/ holds business logic and calls an Adapter interface for all DB access. The Adapter interface is defined in <domain>/adapter.go alongside the Service interface and is implemented by Ent-backed structs in <domain>/adapter/ sub-packages. Service interfaces are composed of fine-grained sub-interfaces (ProfileService, InvoiceService, etc.) so callers depend on the smallest surface they need.
examples: openmeter/billing/service.go – Service composed of ProfileService, InvoiceService, GatheringInvoiceService, LockableService, ConfigService, etc.
openmeter/billing/adapter.go – Adapter composed of ProfileAdapter, InvoiceLineAdapter, GatheringInvoiceAdapter, SequenceAdapter, etc.

patterns.name: Wire-based Dependency Injection
patterns.when_to_use: Wiring together all runtime components of each binary. Each cmd/<binary>/wire.go declares a wire.Build call; provider sets live in app/common/.
patterns.how_it_works: Google Wire generates cmd/<binary>/wire_gen.go at build time. Reusable provider sets (e.g. common.Billing, common.Notification, common.LedgerStack) are declared as wire.NewSet() in app/common/ files and compose individual factory functions. Wire resolves the dependency graph automatically. Each Application struct in cmd/<binary>/wire.go lists every needed service as a field and Wire auto-wires them.
examples: cmd/billing-worker/wire.go – wire.Build combining common.Billing, common.Streaming, common.Watermill, etc.
app/common/billing.go – var Billing = wire.NewSet(BillingAdapter, NewBillingRatingService, NewBillingRegistry, ...)
app/common/notification.go – var Notification = wire.NewSet(NewNotificationAdapter, NewNotificationService, NewNotificationWebhookHandler, ...)

patterns.name: Watermill Message Bus (Kafka-backed publish/subscribe)
patterns.when_to_use: Async domain-event delivery between services. Used for subscription lifecycle events, billing invoice advance events, ingest flush events, and balance-worker recalculation events.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/watermill/eventbus/eventbus.go wraps the Watermill CQRS EventBus. Events are Go structs implementing marshaler.Event (carrying EventName() and EventMetadata()). The Publisher routes events to Kafka topics by inspecting event-name prefixes. Worker processes subscribe via Watermill's Kafka subscriber and dispatch to typed handlers registered in a NoPublishingHandler (openmeter/watermill/grouphandler/grouphandler.go). Handlers are closures matching event type via marshaler; unmatched events are silently ignored. Three topic channels exist: IngestEventsTopic, BalanceWorkerEventsTopic, SystemEventsTopic.
examples: openmeter/watermill/eventbus/eventbus.go – Publisher.Publish routes by name prefix
openmeter/watermill/grouphandler/grouphandler.go – NoPublishingHandler dispatches to typed handlers keyed by CloudEvent type name
openmeter/billing/worker/worker.go – billing worker registers subscription.CreatedEvent, billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent, charges.AdvanceChargesEvent handlers
openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker/worker.go – balance worker registers ingest batch + snapshot events

patterns.name: ServiceHook Registry (observer chain)
patterns.when_to_use: Cross-domain lifecycle callbacks without circular imports. Used by customer.Service, subscription.Service, and billing.Service (StandardInvoiceHooks).
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/models/servicehook.go defines a generic ServiceHook[T] interface (PreUpdate, PreDelete, PostCreate, PostUpdate, PostDelete) and a thread-safe ServiceHookRegistry[T] that fans out to all registered hooks. Loop prevention: a context key unique to the registry prevents re-entrant invocations. Subscription uses a separate SubscriptionCommandHook interface (BeforeCreate, AfterCreate, etc.) in openmeter/subscription/hook.go that is registered via Service.RegisterHook().

patterns.name: Customer RequestValidator Registry
patterns.when_to_use: Pre-request validation for customer mutation operations where billing or subscription constraints must be checked before the customer is modified or deleted.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/customer/requestvalidator.go defines RequestValidator interface with ValidateDeleteCustomer, ValidateCreateCustomer, ValidateUpdateCustomer. A requestValidatorRegistry fans out to all registered validators using errors.Join. billing.validators/customer constructs a billingcustomer.Validator that checks billing pre-conditions and registers it via customerService.RegisterRequestValidator().
examples: app/common/billing.go – customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(validator) after validator construction in NewBillingRegistry

patterns.name: Invoice State Machine (stateless library)
patterns.when_to_use: Driving the invoice lifecycle transitions for StandardInvoice and for charge-level state machines.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/billing/service/stdinvoicestate.go builds a *stateless.StateMachine instance from sync.Pool with external storage bound to the InvoiceStateMachine struct's Invoice.Status field. Transitions trigger actions (DB save, event publish). openmeter/billing/charges/statemachine/machine.go provides a generic Machine[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS] wrapping stateless for charge lifecycles. Both use FireAndActivate + AdvanceUntilStateStable to run all allowed auto-transitions.
examples: openmeter/billing/stdinvoice.go – StandardInvoiceStatus constants (draft.created, draft.collecting, issuing.syncing, issued, payment_processing.pending, paid, etc.)

patterns.name: LineEngine Plugin Registry
patterns.when_to_use: Dispatching billing line calculation to the correct engine (standard invoice, charge flatfee, charge usagebased, charge creditpurchase) based on LineEngineType discriminator.
patterns.how_it_works: billing.LineEngine interface declares GetLineEngineType() + OnCollectionCompleted + OnStandardInvoiceCreated + OnInvoiceIssued + OnPaymentAuthorized + OnPaymentSettled. billing.Service exposes RegisterLineEngine / DeregisterLineEngine. billingservice.engineRegistry (openmeter/billing/service/lineengine.go) stores a map[LineEngineType]LineEngine under a RWMutex. Each charge type implements its own Engine and registers it at startup.
examples: openmeter/billing/lineengine.go – LineEngineType constants and LineEngine interface

patterns.name: App Factory / Registry (external billing app protocol)
patterns.when_to_use: Plugging Stripe, Sandbox, and CustomInvoicing billing apps into the billing state machine without hardcoding them.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/app/registry.go defines AppFactory (NewApp, UninstallApp) and RegistryItem (Listing + Factory). App service (openmeter/app/service.go) exposes RegisterMarketplaceListing / InstallMarketplaceListing. Installed apps must implement openmeter/billing/app.go InvoicingApp interface (ValidateStandardInvoice, UpsertStandardInvoice, FinalizeStandardInvoice, DeleteStandardInvoice). Optionally implement InvoicingAppPostAdvanceHook and InvoicingAppAsyncSyncer for post-advance callbacks and async sync support. app.GetApp() type-asserts installed App to InvoicingApp at runtime.

patterns.name: Entutils TransactingRepo (context-propagated transactions)
patterns.when_to_use: All DB adapter methods that must run inside a caller-supplied transaction or start their own.
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/framework/entutils/transaction.go defines TransactingRepo[R,T] and TransactingRepoWithNoValue[T]. They read the *TxDriver from context (GetDriverFromContext). If one is found, the adapter's WithTx(ctx, tx) method creates a txClient from the raw Ent config. If none is found the adapter runs on its Self() client and starts a new transaction via the Creator interface. Savepoints are used for nested calls so partial rollback is possible.

patterns.name: Locker (pg_advisory_xact_lock)
patterns.when_to_use: Distributed mutual exclusion for per-customer billing operations to prevent concurrent invoice generation races.
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/framework/lockr/locker.go calls pg_advisory_xact_lock($1) with a CRC64-based hash of the lock key. Requires an active Postgres transaction in context (fails otherwise). billing.Service.WithLock acquires the lock per CustomerID before any invoice or charge mutation.

patterns.name: httptransport Operation/Handler pattern
patterns.when_to_use: HTTP endpoint handlers in domain httpdriver packages that separate request decoding, business logic, and response encoding.
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/framework/transport/httptransport/handler.go defines Handler[Request, Response] wrapping an operation.Operation[Request, Response] (a func(ctx, req) (resp, err)). Decoding and encoding are injected via RequestDecoder and ResponseEncoder function types. ErrorEncoders form a chain; the first one to return true short-circuits. The Chain method wraps the operation with operation.Middleware for cross-cutting concerns.
examples: pkg/framework/operation/operation.go – Operation type and Compose / Chain helpers
pkg/framework/commonhttp/encoder.go – GenericErrorEncoder mapping domain errors to HTTP status codes

patterns.name: Subscription Sync Reconciler
patterns.when_to_use: Crash-recovery for the event-driven billing sync: periodically re-syncs subscriptions that may have missed their events.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsync/reconciler/reconciler.go iterates customers/subscriptions in windows and calls subscriptionsync.Service.SynchronizeSubscriptionAndInvoiceCustomer for each. BillingWorker.Worker.AddHandler allows external code to attach additional event handlers after construction.

patterns.name: Namespace multi-tenancy via context + static decoder
patterns.when_to_use: Tenant isolation in every API handler. All service calls require a namespace string.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/namespace/namespace.go defines Manager which fans out CreateNamespace / DeleteNamespace to all registered Handler implementations. Each HTTP server uses namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder to inject the default namespace for self-hosted deployments. Namespace enforcement is structural (namespace field on every input type) rather than middleware-enforced.
examples: openmeter/server/server.go – namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder used in v3 server config

patterns.name: Chi middleware chain + OpenAPI validator
patterns.when_to_use: HTTP request validation and authentication on the v1 REST API.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/server/server.go builds a Chi router with middleware stacks: RealIP, RequestID, request logger, Recoverer, then Authenticator middleware (portal token or API key), then oapi-codegen's OapiRequestValidatorWithOptions against the generated swagger spec (authentication delegated to Nooop so it runs after auth middleware). The v3 API uses a parallel stack with oasmiddleware.ValidateRequest for schema validation.

patterns.name: Noop implementations for optional features
patterns.when_to_use: When a feature is disabled (credits.enabled=false, svix not configured) production code still wires a real interface, but backed by no-op implementations.
patterns.how_it_works: app/common/ledger.go and app/common/notification.go check configuration flags and return noop structs (ledgernoop.Ledger{}, ledgernoop.AccountResolver{}, webhooknoop.New()) so the rest of the wiring does not need to branch. app/common/ledger.go also uses type assertion against noop types to skip namespace handler registration.
examples: app/common/ledger.go – NewLedgerAccountService returns ledgernoop.AccountService{} when credits disabled
app/common/notification.go – NewNotificationWebhookHandler falls back to webhooknoop when svixClient is nil
app/common/notification.go – NotificationService wire set uses NewNoopNotificationWebhookHandler

patterns.name: Sink Worker (Kafka → ClickHouse batch flush)
patterns.when_to_use: High-throughput ingestion path: raw CloudEvents arrive via Kafka, are buffered, deduplicated (Redis or in-memory), and batch-inserted into ClickHouse.
patterns.how_it_works: openmeter/sink/sink.go consumes Kafka partitions via confluent-kafka-go. A SinkBuffer accumulates messages; flush is triggered by MinCommitCount or MaxCommitWait. After flush, openmeter/sink/storage.go ClickHouseStorage.BatchInsert writes to ClickHouse via streaming.Connector.BatchInsert. FlushEventHandlers (openmeter/sink/flushhandler/) are called post-flush for downstream notifications.
examples: openmeter/sink/sink.go – Sink struct, SinkConfig, buffer-based flush loop
openmeter/ingest/kafkaingest/collector.go – Kafka producer used by API ingest handler

patterns.name: ValidationIssue structured error propagation
patterns.when_to_use: Domain-level validation errors that must carry field paths, severity (critical/warning), component names, and arbitrary attributes through service layer boundaries.
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/models/validationissue.go defines ValidationIssue as a value type implementing error. ValidationIssues ([]ValidationIssue) can be converted to/from joined errors. The HTTP layer (commonhttp.HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown) reads the httpStatusCodeErrorAttribute attribute to produce the correct HTTP status. Billing-domain uses billing.ValidationIssue (a different struct) in the invoicing state machine for storing issues on the invoice record.

patterns.name: RFC 7807 Problem Details HTTP response
patterns.when_to_use: All error responses from the REST API.
patterns.how_it_works: pkg/models/problem.go defines Problem interface and StatusProblem struct serialized to application/problem+json. NewStatusProblem reads the request-id from context and sets it as instance URI. Context cancellation is mapped to 408. InternalServerError suppresses the detail string. Extensions map is used by the validation error encoder to attach validationErrors array.
examples: openmeter/server/server.go – r.NotFound and r.MethodNotAllowed use NewStatusProblem

integrations.purpose: Primary relational store for all domain entities: billing profiles, invoices, customers, subscriptions, entitlements, notification channels, ledger accounts, etc.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/ent/schema/ (source of truth); generated code in openmeter/ent/db/; Atlas migrations in tools/migrate/migrations/. Accessed via entdb.Client injected through Wire. Transactions managed by pkg/framework/entutils.TransactingRepo.

integrations.purpose: Append-only analytics store for raw usage events; queried for meter aggregations (count, sum, max, unique_count) via SQL builders.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/streaming/clickhouse/ – event_query.go and meter_query.go build ClickHouse SQL via sqlbuilder. Connector interface in openmeter/streaming/connector.go. ClickHouseStorage in openmeter/sink/storage.go for batch inserts.

integrations.purpose: Durable event bus for domain events (subscription lifecycle, invoice advance, ingest flush notifications, balance recalculation) and raw usage event ingestion.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/watermill/driver/kafka/ – Publisher and Subscriber wrappers. Topic provisioning via KafkaTopicProvisioner in app/common. confluent-kafka-go used directly in openmeter/sink/sink.go for high-throughput ingest consumer.

integrations.purpose: Optional deduplication store for ingest events (preventing double-counting on retry).
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/dedupe/redisdedupe/redisdedupe.go – Redis-backed Deduplicator. In-memory fallback in openmeter/dedupe/memorydedupe/.

integrations.purpose: Outbound webhook delivery for notification events (entitlement balance thresholds, invoice events).
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/notification/webhook/svix/svix.go – Svix API client wrapper. Registered event types passed to Svix application; messages routed by channel filter (NullChannel sentinel prevents unfiltered delivery). Handler interface in openmeter/notification/webhook/handler.go with noop fallback when Svix is unconfigured.

integrations.purpose: Invoice syncing (upsert draft, finalize, collect payment) and customer sync for billing-enabled namespaces.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/app/stripe/app.go implements billing.InvoicingApp (UpsertStandardInvoice, FinalizeStandardInvoice, DeleteStandardInvoice). Stripe REST client in openmeter/app/stripe/client/. App registered via app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing at startup.

integrations.purpose: No-op invoicing app used in development/testing to drive invoice state machine without external dependencies.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/app/sandbox/app.go implements billing.InvoicingApp + InvoicingAppPostAdvanceHook. Registered as marketplace listing with type AppTypeSandbox.

integrations.purpose: Webhook-driven invoicing app allowing external systems to receive invoice payloads and async-confirm sync completion.
integrations.integration_point: openmeter/app/custominvoicing/ – App implements InvoicingApp + InvoicingAppAsyncSyncer; factory in custominvoicing/factory.go.

integrations.purpose: Currency and numeric type library used throughout billing and subscription for currency-safe arithmetic and ISO 4217 currency code validation.
integrations.integration_point: Imported as github.com/invopop/gobl/currency and github.com/invopop/gobl/num in productcatalog, subscription, billing, cost, and currencies packages.

integrations.purpose: Distributed tracing and metrics across all services.
integrations.integration_point: trace.Tracer injected via Wire into service constructors. OTel metric.Meter used in grouphandler (watermill.grouphandler.*) and sink worker. app/common/telemetry.go bootstraps OTLP exporters.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Adding a new domain capability (e.g. a new billing sub-feature)
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Define a new sub-interface in <domain>/service.go, implement it in <domain>/service/<file>.go, add corresponding adapter methods to <domain>/adapter.go and implement in <domain>/adapter/. Wire together in app/common/<domain>.go.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Triggering side-effects on domain object lifecycle (e.g. sync billing on subscription update)
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Avoids circular imports. Billing registers a hook into subscription.Service.RegisterHook() during wiring, not at compile time.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Pre-validating a customer mutation from another domain
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: billing/validators/customer implements RequestValidator and registers via customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(), keeping billing constraints out of the customer package.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Processing async domain events between services
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Events are published to Kafka via eventbus.Publisher and consumed by worker processes that register typed closures. Unknown event types are silently dropped, so workers are tolerant of schema evolution.

The state machine enforces valid transitions and fires actions (DB save, event publish, external app calls) atomically within the transition. Generic Machine[CHARGE,BASE,STATUS] is reused for all charge types.
Event loss is mitigated by a periodic scan of all active subscriptions; SynchronizeSubscriptionAndInvoiceCustomer is idempotent.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Multiple billing backend implementations (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing)
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: New billing backends implement billing.InvoicingApp and register a factory with app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing(). No billing service code changes needed.

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Disabling a subsystem (credits off, no Svix)
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Wire provider functions check config flags and return noops; the rest of the DI graph is unaffected, avoiding nil checks scattered through business logic.
Advisory locks are transactional and released automatically on commit/rollback, avoiding stale lock cleanup. lockr.Locker requires an active Postgres transaction in context.

Domain errors (GenericNotFoundError → 404, GenericValidationError → 400, etc.) are matched by type in GenericErrorEncoder. ValidationIssues with explicit HTTP status attributes are handled first. All errors render as application/problem+json.
High-throughput events flow Kafka → SinkBuffer → ClickHouse in micro-batches. Redis deduplication prevents double-counting on consumer restarts.
Svix handles fan-out, retry, signature verification, and delivery status. The noop implementation runs in tests or when Svix is unconfigured.

pattern_selection.new billing backend: Implement billing.InvoicingApp + AppFactory, register via app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing
pattern_selection.optional feature disabled: Return noop implementation in Wire provider function when config flag is false
pattern_selection.per-customer serialization: billing.Service.WithLock → lockr.Locker.LockForTX (pg advisory lock in tx)
pattern_selection.batch usage ingestion: confluent-kafka-go consumer in Sink worker, ClickHouseStorage.BatchInsert
pattern_selection.DI wiring: Google Wire: wire.NewSet in app/common/, wire.Build in cmd/<binary>/wire.go

error_mapping.description: Entity lookup by ID returned no result. Matched by HandleErrorIfTypeMatches in GenericErrorEncoder.
Input validation failed (missing field, invalid value). Produced via models.NewGenericValidationError or models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.
Duplicate or conflicting resource state. Matched by HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
Operation is not permitted for the caller. Matched by HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
Feature not yet implemented. Matched by HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
Pre-condition not met (e.g. wrong invoice state for the requested operation). Matched by HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
A dependency (external app, downstream service) failed. Not mapped in GenericErrorEncoder; callers wrap it manually.

Structured validation issue with an explicit HTTP status embedded as an attribute; handled first by HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown before the generic error chain. Status is the value stored in the attribute.
Billing-domain specific typed errors mapped in domain httpdriver errors.go files via HandleErrorIfTypeMatches chains (400 for validation, 404 for not-found, 409 for update-after-delete).

error_mapping.error: context canceled (any error with 'context canceled' substring)
error_mapping.description: Detected inside NewStatusProblem; mapped to Request Timeout regardless of original status code.

error_mapping.error: internal server error (unrecognized by any encoder)
error_mapping.description: Falls through all error encoders; StatusProblem with empty detail string is written to prevent leaking internals.

templates.description: Create a new domain service package under openmeter/ following the layered service/adapter pattern with constructor, input types, service interface, and adapter.
Bootstrap a new cmd/ worker binary with Cobra entrypoint, Viper config loading, Wire DI wiring, and Air hot-reload config.
Add a new API resource to the TypeSpec spec, regenerate OpenAPI, and add the Go handler.
Add a new Ent entity schema, run make generate to regenerate ent/db/, then atlas migrate to create SQL migration files.
Define a new notification event type with payload struct and register it in the notification pipeline.
Create a new billing app integration (e.g. a new payment provider) under openmeter/app/.

deployment.runtime_environment: Linux containers (Alpine-based Docker images), deployable on Kubernetes via Helm charts or locally via Docker Compose
compute_services: ghcr.io/openmeterio/openmeter (main API server + all workers in single image)
ci_cd: .github/workflows/ci.yaml — build, lint (go/api-spec/openapi/helm), test, e2e on every push/PR; uses Nix .#ci shell on Depot runners
.github/workflows/release.yaml — publishes Docker images, Helm charts, npm @openmeter/sdk, Python SDK on version tags; JS SDK beta on main push
.github/workflows/artifacts.yaml — reusable workflow: builds and pushes container image to GHCR using Depot depot-build-push-action; signs with cosign
.github/workflows/npm-release.yaml — reusable workflow: publishes @openmeter/sdk to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
.github/workflows/security.yaml — Trufflehog secret scanning on PRs and main pushes
.github/workflows/analysis-scorecard.yaml — OpenSSF Scorecard analysis weekly and on main push
.github/workflows/sdk-python-dev-release.yaml — Python SDK beta release on main push
.github/workflows/require-all-reviewers.yml — enforces all requested reviewers approve before merge
.github/workflows/untrusted-artifacts.yaml — reusable workflow: builds container image without publishing (PR safety)
distribution: Docker images: ghcr.io/openmeterio/openmeter and ghcr.io/openmeterio/benthos-collector (published to GitHub Container Registry on tag)
npm: @openmeter/sdk (JavaScript SDK, published via OIDC trusted publishing on release/beta)
PyPI: openmeter Python SDK (published via sdk-python-dev-release workflow on main and via release)
Helm charts: deploy/charts/openmeter and deploy/charts/benthos-collector (packaged and pushed to GHCR OCI registry on tag)
deployment.infrastructure_as_code: Helm charts in deploy/charts/ (openmeter, benthos-collector); no Terraform found
supporting_services: PostgreSQL 14 — primary datastore (docker-compose.base.yaml: postgres:14.20-alpine; local: localhost:5432)
Kafka (Confluent Platform 8.0.3) — event streaming backbone (docker-compose.base.yaml: confluentinc/cp-kafka:8.0.3)
Redis 7.4.7 — deduplication cache and Svix queue/cache backend (docker-compose.base.yaml, optional profile: redis)
Svix v1.84.1 — webhook delivery server (docker-compose.base.yaml, optional profile: webhook; depends on Postgres + Redis)
Kafka UI (kafbat) — dev-only Kafka management UI (docker-compose.base.yaml, dev profile)
CH-UI — dev-only ClickHouse management UI (docker-compose.base.yaml, dev profile)
deployment.environment_config: Viper-based YAML config loaded from config.yaml (copied from config.example.yaml); key top-level sections: address, telemetry, server, postgres, billing, credits, apps (Stripe baseURL), events, notification, svix, sink (Kafka), ingest (Kafka), dedupe (Redis), progressManager (Redis), portal, meters; autoMigrate controls DB migration strategy at startup

development_rules.rule: Never edit files with a 'Code generated by X, DO NOT EDIT.' header — these include openmeter/ent/db/, **/wire_gen.go, **/convert.gen.go, billing/derived.gen.go, api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go, api/client/go/client.gen.go
Always run 'make generate' (go generate ./...) after editing Ent schemas, Wire providers, or Goverter converter interfaces before building or testing
Always run 'make gen-api' after editing TypeSpec files in api/spec/ to regenerate OpenAPI spec and SDKs before running 'make generate'
development_rules.source: AGENTS.md (Workflow for changing the API) + Makefile (gen-api target)

development_rules.rule: When adding @query or other HTTP decorators to a TypeSpec file that does not already use them, import @typespec/http and add 'using TypeSpec.Http;' in that file — otherwise compilation fails
After editing Ent schema, run 'make generate' then 'atlas migrate --env local diff <name>' to create timestamped .up.sql/.down.sql files and update atlas.sum — all three files must be committed
Never manually edit files in tools/migrate/migrations/ — they are generated by Atlas and validated by CI via 'make migrate-check'
Always build with '-tags=dynamic' (GO_BUILD_FLAGS) to link confluent-kafka-go against the local librdkafka — omitting this tag causes build failures
Always set POSTGRES_HOST=127.0.0.1 when running tests that touch the database — without it many test suites will be skipped even if PostgreSQL is running
development_rules.source: AGENTS.md (Testing section) + Makefile (test target: POSTGRES_HOST=127.0.0.1 go test ...)

development_rules.rule: Start PostgreSQL before running tests: 'docker compose up -d postgres'; the make test target checks PostgreSQL availability and exits with an error if it is not running
Use 't.Context()' instead of 'context.Background()' in tests when a *testing.T or testing.TB is available to keep cancellation tied to the test harness

Keep domain test helpers under openmeter/.../testutils independent from app/common — build test dependencies from underlying package constructors instead of importing application wiring to avoid import cycles
Use 'nix develop --impure .#ci -c <command>' when Go, gofmt, or other toolchain binaries are missing from the ambient shell — Codex may not auto-load .envrc
Never introduce 'context.Background()' or 'context.TODO()' to work around missing context propagation in application code — always propagate the caller's context through the full call path

In openmeter/billing/charges/.../adapter, always wrap Ent access with entutils.TransactingRepo(...) / TransactingRepoWithNoValue(...) even in shared helpers so the helper rebinds to the transaction already carried in ctx

credits.enabled=false must be guarded at multiple layers: ledger-backed credit handlers in api/v3/server, customer ledger hooks, and namespace/default-account provisioning — each must be independently disabled
Register namespace handlers before initNamespace(...) in cmd/server/main.go if they must provision the default namespace during startup
CI enforces lint-go (golangci-lint), lint-api-spec (prettier + tsp), lint-openapi (spectral), and lint-helm on every pull request — all must pass before merge
Every pull request must have exactly one release-note label (release-note/ignore, kind/feature, release-note/bug-fix, etc.) — the PR Checks workflow enforces this
All PRs and main-branch pushes are scanned for secrets using Trufflehog — fail_on_findings is set to true

Schemas declared with ent.View may not appear in migrate.Tables — if 'atlas migrate --env local diff' reports no changes for a new view, add explicit SQL migration manually until generator support is added

architectural_style.title: Multi-binary Go monolith with Kafka-driven async pipelines and TypeSpec-generated REST surface
architectural_style.chosen: A single Go monorepo producing seven deployable binaries (cmd/server, cmd/billing-worker, cmd/balance-worker, cmd/sink-worker, cmd/notification-service, cmd/jobs, cmd/benthos-collector) that all import the same domain packages under openmeter/. Binaries are assembled via Google Wire provider sets in app/common. Kafka (via Watermill + confluent-kafka-go) is the cross-binary event bus (ingest, system, balance topics). Postgres through Ent ORM is the system of record; ClickHouse is the analytics/usage store. HTTP is served by Chi with OpenAPI stubs generated from TypeSpec (api/spec/) into both v1 (openmeter/server/router + api/api.gen.go) and v3 (api/v3/server + api/v3/api.gen.go).
architectural_style.rationale: Shared domain code with independent deployables lets OpenMeter scale hot paths (ingest sink, balance recalculation, billing advancement) horizontally and isolate failure domains while keeping a single compile-time type system for billing correctness. TypeSpec as single source-of-truth lets one spec drive Go server stubs + Go client + JavaScript SDK + Python SDK without drift. Ent + Atlas ties schema to typed Go code with deterministic migration generation. Watermill decouples domain event producers from consumers so the billing-worker / balance-worker / notification-service can evolve independently.
alternatives_rejected: Microservices-per-domain: rejected to avoid N-way API contract duplication across billing/entitlement/subscription and to keep cross-domain invariants (e.g. subscription <-> invoice line sync) in in-process Go types.
Hand-written OpenAPI: rejected because three SDKs (Go, JavaScript, Python) plus dual-versioned server stubs (v1 + v3) would drift; TypeSpec centralizes contract evolution.
sqlc/raw SQL: rejected in favor of Ent to get schema-as-Go-struct with compile-time-checked relations and Atlas-driven migration diffs.
HTTP/RPC between workers: rejected in favor of Kafka topics to absorb ingest bursts, enable replay, and preserve backpressure during billing spikes.

key_decisions.title: TypeSpec as the single source of truth for the HTTP API
key_decisions.chosen: API is authored in TypeSpec under api/spec/packages/ (aip/ for v3, legacy/ for v1) and compiled to OpenAPI YAMLs, Go server stubs (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go), Go client (api/client/go/client.gen.go), and the JavaScript/Python SDKs. `make gen-api` regenerates specs and SDKs; `make generate` then regenerates Go code that depends on them.
key_decisions.rationale: One spec feeds three SDKs and two server versions. Drift between languages is eliminated and the two-step pipeline (TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> generators) is deterministic.
alternatives_rejected: Hand-written OpenAPI
Code-first (generating OpenAPI from Go handlers)
gRPC/Protobuf (would not match REST-first customer expectations and Svix webhook payloads)
key_decisions.forced_by: Multiple SDK languages (Go, JavaScript, Python) + dual API versions (v1 + v3)
key_decisions.enables: Contract-stable breaking-change detection, shared request/response validation via kin-openapi middleware, parallel SDK evolution

key_decisions.title: Ent ORM + Atlas migrations as the schema pipeline
key_decisions.chosen: Ent schemas under openmeter/ent/schema/ are the single source of truth for DB shape. `make generate` produces openmeter/ent/db/. `atlas migrate --env local diff <name>` produces timestamped up/down SQL into tools/migrate/migrations/ plus atlas.sum. Migrations run at server startup when postgres.autoMigrate is set.
key_decisions.rationale: Atlas diffs the Ent schema against the migration history to produce deterministic SQL; Ent gives typed queries and auto-wires relations. Combined they prevent hand-rolled migration drift while keeping Go-native schema definitions.
alternatives_rejected: sqlc (schema still hand-rolled SQL)
GORM (weaker typing, no native schema diff)
Raw golang-migrate only (no typed Go entities)
key_decisions.forced_by: Billing correctness + multi-tenant schema invariants that need compile-time checks
key_decisions.enables: Typed relations across ~60 domain entities, deterministic migration review in PRs

key_decisions.title: Multi-binary deployment sharing a single domain package tree
key_decisions.chosen: Seven cmd/* entry points each call their own Wire-generated initializeApplication from app/common. Domain packages under openmeter/ have no dependency on cmd/* or app/common.
key_decisions.rationale: Ingest throughput, balance recalculation, billing advancement, and notification dispatch have different scaling profiles and failure characteristics. Splitting them into independent binaries while sharing types keeps operational flexibility without fracturing the domain model.
alternatives_rejected: Single monolith binary with goroutine workers (couples failure domains)
Independent microservices with separate repos (fragments shared types and migrations)
key_decisions.forced_by: High-volume per-tenant usage metering with strict billing correctness and independent scaling needs for ingest vs invoicing
key_decisions.enables: Horizontal scaling of sink-worker independent of billing-worker; isolated deploy cadence

key_decisions.title: Kafka + Watermill as the asynchronous event backbone
key_decisions.chosen: openmeter/watermill wraps confluent-kafka-go behind a Watermill Publisher with three named topics (ingest events, system events, balance worker events). Producers call eventbus.Publisher; consumers (billing-worker, balance-worker, notification-service, sink-worker) subscribe via watermill/router with OTel tracing.
key_decisions.rationale: Topic isolation matches the worker topology. Watermill gives per-message retries, dead-lettering, and a uniform router abstraction across four independent binaries. confluent-kafka-go is chosen for librdkafka throughput despite the dynamic-build cost.
alternatives_rejected: Pure confluent-kafka-go without Watermill (loses uniform routing)
NATS/Redis Streams (lower replay semantics, less ecosystem for billing patterns)
Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY (wrong durability/backpressure model for ingest volumes)
key_decisions.forced_by: Ingest bursts + cross-worker side-effects (ingest -> balance recalc -> notification)

key_decisions.title: Google Wire DI concentrated in app/common
key_decisions.chosen: Every domain package exposes plain constructors; all wiring lives in app/common/*.go with Wire provider sets (billing.go, customer.go, entitlement.go, subscription.go, database.go, app.go, notification.go, openmeter_server.go, openmeter_billingworker.go, etc.). cmd/<binary>/wire.go lists the sets; cmd/<binary>/wire_gen.go is generated.
key_decisions.rationale: Declarative wiring with compile-time verification. Changing a constructor signature causes the Wire regeneration step to surface missing providers before runtime.
alternatives_rejected: Manual DI in each cmd (duplicated graphs)
Reflection DI (runtime failure mode incompatible with billing correctness)
Functional options everywhere (no compile-time proof that the full app is buildable)
key_decisions.forced_by: Seven binaries sharing dozens of providers
key_decisions.enables: Single edit point to add a new dependency to any binary

key_decisions.title: credits.enabled feature flag enforced at multiple independent wiring layers
key_decisions.chosen: When credits.enabled is false, app/common wires ledger account services/resolvers to noop implementations AND v3 server ledger-backed customer credit handlers AND customer ledger hooks AND namespace default-account provisioning must each be guarded independently.
key_decisions.rationale: Credits touches several unrelated call graphs (HTTP handlers, customer hooks, namespace provisioning, charge creation). No single choke point can gate it.
alternatives_rejected: Single global flag branch (does not stop ledger writes originating from hooks)
Dynamic runtime check inside ledger.Ledger (performance + correctness risk; writes still attempted)
key_decisions.enables: Credits-disabled tenants genuinely skip ledger writes when every layer is guarded correctly

key_decisions.title: Charges realization with explicit TransactingRepo discipline
key_decisions.chosen: openmeter/billing/charges owns usage-based, flat-fee, and credit-purchase charges. Lifecycle is driven through charges.Service.Create / AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches. Adapter helpers that accept a raw *entdb.Client must wrap their body with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound transaction is honored.
key_decisions.rationale: Charge advancement mixes reads, realization runs, and ledger-bound writes. Without explicit TransactingRepo rebinding in every helper, a helper can fall off the transaction and cause partial writes under concurrency.
alternatives_rejected: Pass *entdb.Tx explicitly (leaks tx plumbing into every call site)
Global tx middleware (cannot enforce per-helper without compiler help)
key_decisions.forced_by: Ent transactions carried implicitly in ctx
key_decisions.enables: Deterministic multi-step charge advancement without partial writes

key_decisions.title: Dynamic build tag for librdkafka (GO_BUILD_FLAGS=-tags=dynamic)
key_decisions.chosen: All binaries and test invocations build with -tags=dynamic so confluent-kafka-go links against system librdkafka.
key_decisions.rationale: Dynamic linking cuts binary size and speeds tests; matches production librdkafka availability. CI uses nix develop --impure .#ci to pin the toolchain.
alternatives_rejected: Static link (pure go kafka client is lower throughput; static librdkafka is hard to ship)
Sarama (lower Kafka-protocol coverage at the throughput OpenMeter needs)
key_decisions.forced_by: High-volume ingest + tests that touch Kafka
key_decisions.enables: Consistent library behavior across dev, CI, and production images

trade_offs.accept: Ent-generated query friction (longer compile on schema change, boilerplate in openmeter/ent/db/)
trade_offs.benefit: Compile-time-checked relations across ~60 entities, automatic Atlas diffing, no runtime schema surprises
violation_signals: Hand-written SQL added alongside Ent queries
New table created without corresponding openmeter/ent/schema/*.go

trade_offs.accept: Multi-binary orchestration cost (Docker images, Helm values, local docker-compose) and operational complexity
trade_offs.benefit: Horizontal scaling of sink-worker / balance-worker / billing-worker independent of HTTP traffic; fault isolation per binary
trade_offs.caused_by: Multi-binary deployment of cmd/server, cmd/billing-worker, cmd/balance-worker, cmd/sink-worker, cmd/notification-service
violation_signals: Workers added without matching app/common/openmeter_*worker.go Wire set
Cross-binary dependencies introduced through shared global state instead of Kafka topic

trade_offs.accept: Two-step regen cadence: TypeSpec -> `make gen-api`, then `make generate` for Go server/wire/ent
trade_offs.benefit: Stable cross-language SDK contracts; contract drift is impossible as long as both steps run
violation_signals: PRs touching api/spec/ without regenerated api/openapi.yaml or api/v3/openapi.yaml
Client SDKs under api/client/** drifting from api/spec/

trade_offs.accept: librdkafka C dependency (dynamic linking at build time)
trade_offs.benefit: High-throughput Kafka producer/consumer, consistent semantics with Kafka ecosystem
violation_signals: Attempts to switch to a pure-Go Kafka client

trade_offs.accept: Sequential Atlas migration filenames (timestamped .up.sql/.down.sql) can collide on long-running branches
trade_offs.benefit: Deterministic, reviewable SQL migrations with atlas.sum verifying the chain
trade_offs.caused_by: atlas migrate --env local diff naming + atlas.sum chain hashing
violation_signals: Attempts to edit existing migrations after they land

out_of_scope: Frontend UI (frontend_ratio = 0; React only appears in the generated JavaScript SDK under api/client/javascript/)
Business-level auth / identity provider (portal tokens handle end-customer scoping; core tenant auth is outside this repo)
Managed hosting control plane (config.cloud.yaml and api/openapi.cloud.yaml exist but the hosted-platform logic is not in this monorepo)

decision_chain.root: OpenMeter must provide high-volume per-tenant usage metering feeding strict billing correctness, with stable multi-language SDKs.

forces.decision: Multi-binary deployment with shared domain packages
forces.rationale: Ingest, balance recalc, and billing advancement have very different throughput and latency envelopes, but share the billing correctness model.

forces.decision: Kafka + Watermill as the async event backbone
forces.rationale: Multi-binary needs durable message passing with replay and backpressure to connect ingest, workers, and notifications without in-memory coupling.

forces.decision: Three named Kafka topics (ingest, system, balance)
forces.rationale: Isolating ingest from billing system events prevents one hot producer from starving another consumer group.
violation_keywords: mixing ingest events on system topic

forces.decision: Google Wire DI concentrated in app/common
forces.rationale: Each cmd/* binary needs its own provider graph, but every domain service must be wireable identically. Wire makes this compile-time checked.
violation_keywords: manual constructor call chain in cmd/

forces.decision: TypeSpec as the API source of truth
forces.rationale: Multi-language SDKs plus dual server versions force contract-first to avoid drift.
violation_keywords: adding endpoint only in Go handler

forces.decision: Two-step regen: make gen-api then make generate
forces.rationale: OpenAPI is an intermediate; Go server stubs and SDKs are downstream generators.
Billing correctness needs typed relations and reviewable migration diffs.

forces.decision: Sequential timestamped migrations with atlas.sum chain
credits.enabled guarded at multiple wiring layers
forces.rationale: Credits cross-cuts ledger, customer hooks, v3 handlers, and namespace provisioning; no single guard point exists.
violation_keywords: default account provisioned when credits disabled

pitfalls.problem_statement: Charges adapter helpers accepting a raw *entdb.Client bypass the ctx-bound Ent transaction and produce partial writes under concurrency.
evidence: AGENTS.md explicit warning about TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue in openmeter/billing/charges/.../adapter.
openmeter/billing/charges/adapter.go; openmeter/billing/charges/service.go; charges.Service.Create / AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches drive multi-write flows.
pitfalls.root_cause: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; any helper that uses the raw client instead of rebinding falls off the transaction.
fix_direction: Wrap every such helper body with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue.
Codify this as a checklist item in the /charges skill and PR review template.

pitfalls.problem_statement: Disabling credits at the config level does not fully stop ledger writes unless every wiring layer is guarded independently.
evidence: AGENTS.md calls out four distinct layers: api/v3/server ledger-backed credit handlers, customer ledger hooks, namespace/default-account provisioning, and app/common noop fallback.
app/common/customer.go wires customer service with ledger hooks; openmeter/namespace/namespace.go registers handlers including ledger.
openmeter/ledger/customerbalance and openmeter/ledger/account expose write paths.
pitfalls.root_cause: The credits feature is cross-cutting; no single injection point dominates all call graphs that can reach ledger writes.
fix_direction: Enumerate every ledger write path (grep for ledger_accounts / ledger_customer_accounts touches).
Verify each path is behind a Wire-provided noop when credits.enabled=false.
Add a smoke test that boots with credits.enabled=false and asserts ledger tables stay empty.

pitfalls.problem_statement: The multi-generator toolchain (TypeSpec + Ent + Goverter + Wire + Goderive) is easy to leave partially regenerated, producing silent drift between specs, code, and SDKs.
evidence: AGENTS.md generator table lists five independent generators feeding different artifacts (api/*.gen.go, openmeter/ent/db/, **/wire_gen.go, **/convert.gen.go, billing/derived.gen.go).
AGENTS.md workflow mandates both `make gen-api` AND `make generate` for API changes.
cmd/*/wire_gen.go files must be regenerated whenever constructors change.
pitfalls.root_cause: Several generators read different sources and write different outputs; a developer who runs only one of them leaves the repo in an inconsistent state that may compile but behave incorrectly.
fix_direction: Always run `make generate-all` after touching TypeSpec, Ent schema, Wire provider sets, Goverter interfaces, or Goderive-annotated code.
In CI, run the generators and fail if the working tree is dirty.
Document the generator graph in AGENTS.md (already partially present) and keep it in sync.

pitfalls.problem_statement: Sequential Atlas migration filenames + atlas.sum chain hashing guarantee merge collisions on long-lived feature branches.
evidence: AGENTS.md migration workflow (atlas migrate --env local diff) and the /rebase skill description explicitly mention 'sequential migrations, atlas.sum conflicts'.
tools/migrate/migrations/ holds timestamped .up.sql/.down.sql files plus atlas.sum.
pitfalls.root_cause: atlas.sum records a linear hash chain over migration files; two branches both appending migrations produce two different hash chains that cannot merge cleanly.
fix_direction: On rebase, delete the branch's migration files and its atlas.sum lines.
Re-run `make generate` (Ent) then `atlas migrate --env local diff <name>` to regenerate the migration against the rebased schema.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Author endpoints in TypeSpec under api/spec/packages/{aip,legacy}. Run `make gen-api` to regenerate OpenAPI YAML + Go server stubs + Go/JS/Python SDKs, then `make generate` for downstream Go code. Handler packages under openmeter/*/httpdriver (v1) or api/v3/handlers (v3) implement the generated server interface using the generic pkg/framework/transport/httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] adapter and delegate to domain services.
tips: Never hand-edit *.gen.go; always regenerate.
If TypeSpec adds @query and the file lacks HTTP decorators, import @typespec/http and `using TypeSpec.Http;`.
Keep v1 changes in legacy/, v3 in aip/; do not mix.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Ent schemas under openmeter/ent/schema are the source of truth. `make generate` regenerates openmeter/ent/db/. Migrations are produced by `atlas migrate --env local diff <name>` into tools/migrate/migrations/ as timestamped .up.sql/.down.sql plus atlas.sum. Adapters under openmeter/<domain>/adapter implement domain interfaces with Ent queries and must be transaction-aware via entutils.TransactingRepo.
tips: Never edit openmeter/ent/db/; it is generated.
After schema changes: make generate, then atlas migrate --env local diff <name>.
Helpers that take *entdb.Client must still wrap with entutils.TransactingRepo to honor ctx tx.
Ent views may not appear in migrate/schema.go; add explicit SQL migration if atlas reports no changes.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: openmeter/watermill wraps confluent-kafka-go. Publishers route to three topics (ingest, system, balance). Consumers (sink, balance, billing, notification) use watermill/router with OTel tracing. Sink flushes ingest events to ClickHouse, then publishes ingest notifications to trigger balance recalculation.
tips: Always build with -tags=dynamic so librdkafka links.
Use the named topic constant from openmeter/watermill, not string literals.
Carry ctx through the consumer via msg.Context() - do not substitute context.Background().

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: billing.Service is a composite interface (Profile, Invoice, Line, Sequence, etc.) implemented in openmeter/billing/service. openmeter/billing/charges owns charge lifecycle (usage-based, flat-fee, credit-purchase). openmeter/billing/worker runs auto-advance and collect loops; openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsync reconciles subscription views into invoice lines. Invoice format is GOBL.
tips: Test charge lifecycle through charges.Service.Create / AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches, not via low-level adapters.
Use MockStreamingConnector with explicit StoredAt to model late-arriving usage and exercise stored-at cutoff logic.
For integration tests, use billing.BaseSuite + SubscriptionMixin.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: credits.enabled must be honored at four independent layers: (1) app/common wires ledger services to noop when disabled; (2) api/v3/server credit handlers must skip registration; (3) customer ledger hooks must be unregistered; (4) namespace default-account provisioning must skip ledger account creation. A single guard is insufficient.
tips: Any new code that touches ledger_accounts or ledger_customer_accounts must have a credits.enabled=false path that does nothing.
When writing a backfill that genuinely needs ledger writes, build the concrete adapters directly instead of relying on DI defaults (they are noops).
Add a credits-disabled integration test that asserts no ledger table rows are produced.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: openmeter/notification manages channels, rules, events, and delivery status. notification.EventHandler runs Dispatch + Reconcile loops. The Watermill consumer in openmeter/notification/consumer subscribes to the system events topic, builds the payload, and sends it through Svix. Payload version is pinned per event family.
tips: Pin payload version constants per event family and treat them as API contracts.
Reconcile loop is responsible for retrying failed deliveries; don't duplicate retry logic inline.
Boot order: register notification handlers before initNamespace when the default namespace needs them.

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Every entry point (HTTP handlers, Kafka consumers, Ent adapters) is instrumented. ingestadapter wraps openmeter/ingest.Collector with OTel. watermill router construction adds OTel tracing. All domain calls must propagate ctx (no context.Background() / TODO()).
tips: Never substitute context.Background() to work around a missing ctx plumbing issue; fix the caller.
In tests, use t.Context() instead of context.Background() when testing.T is available.
